agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core e-commerce/logistics capability that allows customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment is complete. It intersects payment processing (refunds), inventory management (restocking), and fulfillment operations (stopping in-progress work). The PM's statement is a high-level feature request with no specifics on cancellation windows, refund policies, or order state constraints.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's confirmed request to purchase one or more items, tracked through a lifecycle of states from placement to delivery or cancellation."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current state of an order in its lifecycle (e.g., Pending, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A customer-initiated or system-initiated action that terminates an order before final delivery, triggering refund and inventory reversal processes."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period during which an order is eligible for cancellation — typically before a defined fulfillment milestone."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the customer following a cancellation, which may be full or partial depending on the order's fulfillment state at the time of cancellation."},{"term":"Fulfillment","definition":"The process of picking, packing, and shipping an order to the customer."},{"term":"Restocking","definition":"Returning cancelled order items to available inventory."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order can only be cancelled if it has not yet reached an irreversible fulfillment state (e.g., Delivered).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancelling a delivered order is a physical impossibility within the cancellation domain — it becomes a return, which is a separate bounded context. Allowing cancellation of delivered orders would corrupt order state.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status Delivered, when a cancellation is attempted, then the system must reject the cancellation with an appropriate error."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Only the customer who placed the order (the order owner) may initiate a cancellation.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel others' orders is a data integrity and security violation. This is an ownership constraint, not a policy preference.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancellation request from a user who is not the order owner, when the system processes the request, then it must reject the request with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A cancelled order cannot transition to any state other than Cancelled — cancellation is a terminal state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Re-activating a cancelled order would break audit trails, potentially double-charge customers, and corrupt inventory counts. If the customer wants the same items, they place a new order.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in Cancelled status, when any state transition is attempted, then the system must reject the transition."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Cancellation must trigger a refund process for any payments already captured.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Retaining payment for goods not delivered violates basic transactional integrity and consumer protection law. The refund amount may vary by policy, but the initiation of a refund process is non-negotiable.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancelled order with captured payment, when the cancellation is confirmed, then a refund record must be created for the order."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Cancellation must trigger inventory restoration for all items in the cancelled order.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"While generally true, some businesses may choose not to restock certain items (perishables, custom goods). This is a policy decision, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancelled order with restockable items, when cancellation is confirmed, then each item's available inventory count must increase by the cancelled quantity."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"The system must record a cancellation reason and timestamp for audit purposes.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Audit logging depth is a compliance and business intelligence policy decision, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a completed cancellation, when the cancellation record is inspected, then it must contain a non-null reason, the cancelling user ID, and a UTC timestamp."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has an order in Pending or Confirmed status","when":"The customer requests cancellation of that order","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a refund is initiated for any captured payment, and inventory is restored for all restockable items"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer has an order in Shipped status","when":"The customer requests cancellation of that order","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and informs the customer that the order has already shipped, directing them to the returns process"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an order in Delivered status","when":"The customer requests cancellation of that order","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and informs the customer to use the returns/refund process instead"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer has an order that is already Cancelled","when":"The customer requests cancellation again","then":"The system returns an idempotent success response indicating the order is already cancelled, without triggering duplicate refunds or inventory adjustments"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A user who did not place the order","when":"That user attempts to cancel the order","then":"The system rejects the request with an authorization error and does not modify the order"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer has an order in Processing status (being picked/packed but not yet shipped)","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The system must define whether this is cancellable — this is an ambiguous boundary (see escalations)"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer has an order with multiple items","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The entire order is cancelled — all items are reversed, refund covers all items (partial cancellation behavior is ambiguous, see escalations)"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A cancellation is successfully processed","when":"The system records the cancellation","then":"An audit record is created containing: order ID, cancelling user ID, cancellation reason, UTC timestamp, and refund amount initiated"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A customer requests cancellation and a payment refund fails","when":"The refund processor returns an error","then":"The order remains in a Cancellation Pending state and the system retries or alerts operations — the order must not silently stay cancelled without a refund"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Two concurrent cancellation requests arrive for the same order","when":"Both are processed simultaneously","then":"Exactly one cancellation is processed; the second receives an idempotent response. No double refunds or double inventory restorations occur."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Order is in Processing state — warehouse has started picking items","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous: depends on whether the business defines a cancellation cutoff at order confirmation or at shipment. Must be clarified by PM. If cancellable, warehouse must be notified to halt fulfillment."},{"scenario":"Partial cancellation — customer wants to cancel 2 of 5 items","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous: the PM said 'cancel their order' (singular, whole order). Partial cancellation is a distinct feature with different refund calculations, inventory implications, and UX. Must be explicitly scoped in or out."},{"scenario":"Payment refund fails after order is already marked Cancelled","expected_behavior":"Order should transition to a Cancellation Pending or Refund Failed state. Operations must be alerted. The customer must not see a cancelled order without receiving their refund."},{"scenario":"Order placed with a promotion or discount code","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous: should the promotion/discount be returned to the customer's account for reuse? This depends on business policy for promotional offers."},{"scenario":"Order contains non-cancellable items (custom/personalized goods, perishables already prepared)","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous: the system may need item-level cancellability flags. PM must clarify if any item types are exempt from cancellation."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested during a payment authorization hold (not yet captured)","expected_behavior":"The authorization hold should be voided rather than a refund initiated. Different payment flow than captured payments."},{"scenario":"Concurrent state change — order ships at the exact moment cancellation is requested","expected_behavior":"Race condition: the system must use optimistic locking or equivalent to ensure atomic state transitions. Either the cancellation wins (and shipment is recalled) or the shipment wins (and cancellation is rejected). No inconsistent state allowed."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels and immediately re-orders the same items","expected_behavior":"These must be treated as independent transactions. Inventory freed by the cancellation should be available for the new order, but no guaranteed reservation exists."},{"scenario":"Subscription or recurring order cancellation","expected_behavior":"Out of scope unless PM clarifies. Cancelling a single instance of a recurring order vs cancelling the subscription are fundamentally different operations."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation directly impacts revenue (lost sales), refund processing costs (payment gateway fees may not be refundable), and inventory carrying costs. A well-designed cancellation flow reduces customer service costs by enabling self-service. A poorly designed one risks double refunds, lost inventory, or chargebacks.","operational":"Fulfillment operations must be able to receive and act on cancellation signals before shipment. Late cancellations that arrive after picking has started create warehouse inefficiency. The cancellation window policy directly determines operational complexity.","user":"Cancellation capability is a baseline customer expectation in e-commerce. Lack of self-service cancellation drives support ticket volume and customer dissatisfaction. Overly restrictive cancellation policies drive chargebacks and negative reviews. Overly permissive policies enable abuse.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) double refunds from race conditions, (2) inventory desync from failed restocking, (3) customer disputes from unclear cancellation policies, (4) fraud via cancel-after-ship schemes. All require explicit handling.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain self-service cancellation capability, reducing friction and improving trust. Unclear policies may cause frustration."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced ticket volume for cancellation requests. May see increased inquiries about edge cases (partial cancellation, post-ship cancellation)."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Must integrate cancellation signals into picking/packing workflow. Orders cancelled mid-fulfillment create rework."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Refund processing, revenue recognition adjustments, potential payment gateway fee losses on refunded transactions."},{"group":"Product/PM","impact":"Must define cancellation window policy, partial cancellation scope, and promotion handling — these are business decisions, not engineering decisions."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The PM's spec is a single sentence with no detail on cancellation windows, eligible states, partial cancellation, refund policy, or promotion handling. Multiple critical business decisions are undefined. The acceptance criteria above represent reasonable defaults for e-commerce, but at least 4 significant ambiguities could change the implementation substantially.","high_confidence_areas":["Cancelled is a terminal state","Only the order owner can cancel","Cancellation must trigger a refund process","Delivered orders cannot be cancelled (that is a return)","Idempotency on duplicate cancellation requests"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact set of cancellable order states (especially Processing/in-fulfillment)","Whether partial item cancellation is in scope","Promotion/discount code restoration policy","Non-cancellable item types","Refund amount calculation (full vs prorated vs policy-dependent)"],"assumptions":["This is a standard e-commerce order, not a subscription or recurring order","The system has a well-defined order state machine","Payment capture happens at or before fulfillment, not at delivery","The returns process exists as a separate capability for post-delivery reversals"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The cancellation window is undefined. Which order states allow cancellation? Specifically, can a customer cancel after the order enters Processing (warehouse has begun picking)? The answer has major operational and financial implications — a narrow window (only Pending/Confirmed) is operationally simple but customer-hostile; a wide window (up to Shipped) is customer-friendly but operationally expensive.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Product/PM"],"options":["Allow cancellation only in Pending and Confirmed states","Allow cancellation up to and including Processing state (with warehouse notification)","Allow cancellation until shipment carrier scan"],"recommendation":"Allow cancellation up to Processing with a best-effort warehouse halt. Reject after carrier scan. This balances customer experience with operational feasibility."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial cancellation is not addressed. The PM said 'cancel their order' — is this whole-order only, or can a customer cancel individual line items from a multi-item order? Partial cancellation requires different refund calculations, may affect free-shipping thresholds, and significantly increases implementation complexity.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting","Product/PM"],"options":["Whole-order cancellation only (MVP)","Partial line-item cancellation supported","Whole-order MVP now, partial cancellation as a follow-up feature"],"recommendation":"Ship whole-order cancellation first as MVP. Partial cancellation is a separate feature with distinct domain rules and should be scoped independently."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Refund policy and promotion handling are undefined. When an order placed with a discount code is cancelled, is the discount code restored for reuse? Is the refund always the full amount paid, or can it be reduced (e.g., restocking fees)? These are business policy decisions that materially affect the financial model.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting","Product/PM"],"options":["Full refund of amount paid, promotion code restored","Full refund of amount paid, promotion code consumed","Refund minus restocking fee, promotion code handling TBD"],"recommendation":"Default to full refund of amount paid with promotion code restored. Restocking fees and non-restorable promotions can be added as policy configurations later."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core e-commerce/logistics capability that allows customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment is complete. It intersects payment processing (refunds), inventory management (restocking), and fulfillment operations (stopping in-progress work). The PM's statement is a high-level feature request with no specifics on cancellation windows, refund policies, or order state constraints.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous

content.thinking:
The user wants me to analyze "Users should be able to cancel their order" and produce acceptance criteria. This is a vague spec from a non-technical stakeholder. I need to identify the ambiguities and produce my full JSON output.

Let me classify: This is `ambiguity` — the requirement is vague and can be interpreted multiple ways. "Cancel their order" doesn't specify time windows, refund policies, partial cancellation, etc.

Let me produce the full output.
